evidence: R&D expenses increased primarily due to higher headcount-related expenses reflecting our investments in AI, share-based compensation expense, cash compensation expenses from acquisitions
R&D expenses: $9,300M in fiscal 2025 vs $7,983M in fiscal 2024, a $1,317M (16%) increase, with AI investments cited as primary driver

we also intend to invest in personnel and other resources related to our engineering, sales, service and marketing functions as we realign and dedicate resources on key priority areas, such as AI, cloud, and cybersecurity

We provide network infrastructure to power AI training and inference workloads for both webscale providers and enterprises... high-density routers and switches, improved network management, and high-performance optics

Cisco Hypershield, our cloud-native and AI-powered approach to highly distributed security for AI-scale data centers that is built into the fabric of the network, is the first service offering available embedded on these new switches
revenue increase in our service provider and cloud market was driven by AI infrastructure revenue from webscale customers

AI algorithms and training methodologies may be flawed... AI-related legal and regulatory landscape is constantly evolving... obligations to comply with the evolving legal and regulatory landscape could entail significant costs or limit our ability to incorporate certain AI capabilities
emergence and maturation of AI capabilities has led to new and/or more effective methods of cyber attacks... our solutions and IT environment have been, and continue to be, vulnerable
U.S. copyright or patent protection for inventions or works developed in part or wholly by generative AI tools may be limited, or not available at all

capex_mentions.quote: R&D expenses increased primarily due to higher headcount-related expenses reflecting our investments in AI
capex_mentions.context: Fiscal 2025 R&D expenses of $9,300M, up $1,317M (16%) YoY, with AI investments cited as primary driver

limitations: No explicit dollar amount is broken out solely for AI R&D or AI capex; the $9,300M R&D figure is total R&D with AI cited as a key driver, not a standalone AI-attributable figure — investment intensity scored 4 rather than 5 due to lack of a discrete multi-year AI-specific commitment amount

AI narrative concreteness scored 4 (not 5) because while AI infrastructure revenue growth from webscale customers is mentioned, no specific quantified business results (e.g., percentage of revenue, units deployed) are provided for individual AI use cases

Risk disclosure scored 4 because multiple company-specific risk mechanisms are described (IP protection limits for AI-generated works, regulatory compliance costs, cyber attack vectors enabled by AI) but no quantified risk exposure or named governance body is disclosed
